What is the value mentioned in: ipinas ang kalayaan'
3. Mga siyentipikong katotohanan: 'Ang tubig ay nagyelo sa 0°C'
4. Maaaring ber...
quizzes.explanation: The correct value is 3, as stated in the textbook.

quizzes.q: What is the value mentioned in: ampung taon. Ayon sa isang pag-aaral ng isang malaking kompanya ng teknolohiya, 85% ng mga mag-aaral...
quizzes.explanation: The correct value is 85, as stated in the textbook.

quizzes.q: What is the value mentioned in: ulan ay isang kompanya ng teknolohiya na may interes sa online learning. 2. Ang 85% na datos ay maaa...
quizzes.explanation: The correct value is 2, as stated in the textbook.

quizzes.q: What is the value mentioned in: yuda sa mga apektadong pamilya. Ayon sa PSA, ang unemployment rate ay umabot sa 17.7% noong Abril 20...
quizzes.explanation: The correct value is 17.7, as stated in the textbook.

quizzes.q: What is the value mentioned in: . Facts: nagsimula ang pandemya 2020, nagsara ang eskwelahan, unemployment rate 17.7%. Opinyon: wala...
quizzes.explanation: The correct value is 2020, as stated in the textbook.

quizzes.q: According to the context, what numerical value is correct?
quizzes.explanation: The correct value is 45, as stated in the textbook.

quizzes.q: Which numerical value is accurate based on the source material?
quizzes.explanation: The correct value is 45, as stated in the textbook.

quizzes.q: Ano ang reading comprehension?
opts: Ang kakayahang maunawaan, suriin, at magbigay-kahulugan sa teksto
quizzes.explanation: Reading comprehension — hindi lang pagbasa kundi pag-unawa sa nilalaman, pagsusuri ng teksto, at pagkuha ng kahulugan.

quizzes.q: Ano ang tamang estratehiya sa pagsagot ng reading comprehension questions sa CET?
opts: Basahin muna ang mga tanong bago ang teksto, pagkatapos ay i-skim ang teksto
Basahin ang buong teksto nang paulit-ulit
quizzes.explanation: Estratehiya: (1) Basahin muna ang tanong para malaman kung ano ang hahanapin, (2) I-skim ang teksto, (3) Balikan ang tiyak na bahagi para sa mga sagot.

quizzes.q: Ano ang main idea ng isang talata?
opts: Ang pangunahing punto o argumento na nais iparating ng may-akda
quizzes.explanation: Ang main idea ay ang pangunahing punto o argumento ng teksto. Karaniwang makikita sa unang o huling pangungusap ng talata, pero minsan ay ipinapahiwatig sa buong talata.

quizzes.q: Ano ang pagkakaiba ng explicit at implicit na impormasyon sa teksto?
opts: Explicit = hayagang sinabi; Implicit = ipinapahiwatig lang o kailangang hulaan
quizzes.explanation: Explicit — hayagang nakasulat sa teksto. Implicit — hindi direktang sinabi pero maaaring mapagkailusan mula sa konteksto, tono, o mga pahiwatig.

quizzes.q: Kapag ang teksto ay nagsasabing "Ang edukasyon sa Pilipinas ay may mga positibong pagbabago ngunit may mga hamon pa rin," ano ang tono ng may-akda?
opts: Balanseng pagtatasa — may positibo at negatibo
quizzes.explanation: Ang salitang "ngunit" ay nagpapakita ng balanseng pananaw — kinikilala ang positibo habang inaamin ang mga hamon. Ito ay balanseng pagtatasa.

quizzes.q: Ano ang inference?
opts: Pagkuha ng konklusyon mula sa mga ebidensya sa teksto na hindi direktang sinabi
quizzes.explanation: Inference — pagkuha ng konklusyon batay sa mga ebidensya at pahiwatig sa teksto, kahit hindi ito direktang sinabi ng may-akda.

quizzes.q: Ano ang pagkakaiba ng fact at opinion sa isang teksto?
opts: Fact ay maaaring patunayan; Opinion ay personal na paniniwala o paghatol
Fact ay palaging tama; Opinion ay palaging mali
Opinion ay mas mahalaga kaysa fact
quizzes.explanation: Fact — pahayag na maaaring patunayan o beripikahin (datos, pangyayari). Opinion — personal na paniniwala, paghatol, o damdamin na hindi maaaring patunayan.

quizzes.q: Kapag ang teksto ay gumagamit ng salitang dapat, kailangan, o nararapat, ano ang ipinapahiwatig nito?
opts: Opinion o rekomendasyon ng may-akda, hindi fact
quizzes.explanation: Ang mga salitang "dapat," "kailangan," "nararapat" ay nagpapahiwatig ng opinion o rekomendasyon — hindi ito fact dahil hindi ito maaaring patunayan objectively.

quizzes.q: Ano ang purpose ng transition words sa isang teksto?
opts: Nag-uugnay ng mga ideya sa pagitan ng mga pangungusap o talata
quizzes.explanation: Transition words (hal., gayundin, subalit, samakatuwid) ay nag-uugnay ng mga ideya — nagpapakita ng relasyon (sanhi, pagkakasunod, pagtutol) sa pagitan ng mga pangungusap.

quizzes.q: Ano ang skimming at scanning?
opts: Skimming = mabilis na pagbabasa para sa pangkalahatang ideya; Scanning = paghahanap ng tiyak na impormasyon
quizzes.explanation: Skimming — mabilis na pagbabasa para sa main idea (unang/uling pangungusap). Scanning — paghahanap ng tiyak na datos (pangalan, numero, petsa) nang hindi binabasa ang lahat.

quizzes.q: Ano ang unang hakbang sa pagsasaliksik?
quizzes.explanation: Ang unang hakbang sa pagsasaliksik ay ang pagpili ng paksa. Ito ang pundasyon ng buong saliksik at nagbibigay-direksiyon sa mga susunod na hakbang.

quizzes.q: Ano ang 'bibliography'?
quizzes.explanation: Ang 'bibliography' ay isang listahan ng lahat ng mga pinagmulan na ginamit sa saliksik. Ito ay nagbibigay ng kredito sa mga orihinal na may-akda.

quizzes.q: Ang 'qualitative research' ay gumagamit ng:
quizzes.explanation: Ang 'qualitative research' ay gumagamit ng deskriptiv na datos tulad ng mga interbyu, obserbasyon, at mga kwento upang maunawaan ang mga karanasan at perspektibo.

quizzes.q:
What is the value mentioned in: kad ako.'

Teknikal: 'Ang konduktibiti ng solusyon ay 0.5 S/m sa temperatura na 25°C.'

Sa CET, maha...
quizzes.explanation: The correct value is 0.5, as stated in the textbook.

quizzes.q: What is the value mentioned in: sa ulat ng Philippine Statistics Authority, ang unemployment rate ay bumaba sa 4.5% noong Marso 2026...
quizzes.explanation: The correct value is 4.5, as stated in the textbook.

quizzes.q:
What is the value mentioned in: pot website.
   b. 'Ayon sa Department of Health, ang dengue cases ay tumaas ng 15% sa NCR.' — mula ...
quizzes.explanation: The correct value is 15, as stated in the textbook.

quizzes.q: According to the context, what numerical value is correct?
quizzes.explanation: The correct value is 0.5, as stated in the textbook.

quizzes.q: Which numerical value is accurate based on the source material?
quizzes.explanation: The correct value is 0.5, as stated in the textbook.

quizzes.q: Ano ang thesis statement sa isang sanaysay?
opts: Ang pangunahing pahayag na naglalaman ng posisyon o pangunahing ideya
quizzes.explanation: Ang thesis statement ang pinakamahalagang bahagi ng sanaysay — ito ang pangunahing pahayag na naglalaman ng posisyon o pangunahing ideya ng manunulat.

quizzes.q: Alin ang tamang pagkakasunod-sunod ng bahagi ng sanaysay?
quizzes.explanation: Ang tamang ayos: Panimula (nagpapakilala sa paksa), Katawan (mga argumento at ebidensya), Konklusyon (nagbabalik-loob sa thesis).

quizzes.q: Alin ang pinakamahusay na thesis statement?
opts: Ang labis na paggamit ng social media ay nagdudulot ng pagbaba ng academic performance ng mga mag-aaral.
quizzes.explanation: Magandang thesis statement ay tiyak (specific), may posisyon, at maaaring suportahan ng ebidensya — hindi masyadong malawak o makitid.

quizzes.q: Ano ang tawag sa unang yugto ng proseso ng pagsulat kung saan nag-iisip ng paksa at gumagawa ng outline?
quizzes.explanation: Ang pre-writing ang unang yugto: nag-iisip ng paksa, nagre-research, at gumagawa ng outline bago magsulat ng draft.

quizzes.q: Ano ang liham-pangangalakal?
opts: Pormal na liham para sa mga transaksyon sa negosyo o opisyal na pakikipag-ugnayan
Liham para sa editor ng dyaryo
quizzes.explanation: Ang liham-pangangalakal (business letter) ay isang pormal na liham para sa mga transaksyon sa negosyo o opisyal na pakikipag-ugnayan.

quizzes.q: Alin ang tatlong pangunahing elemento ng komunikasyon?
quizzes.explanation: Tatlong elemento: Sender (nagpadala), Message (mensahe), at Channel (daluyan). Walang komunikasyon kung kulang ang isa.

quizzes.q: Ano ang active listening?
opts: Pakinig na may buong atensyon at layuning maunawaan ang mensahe
quizzes.explanation: Active listening — pakikinig na may buong atensyon at layuning maunawaan ang mensahe. Hindi lang naririnig ang salita kundi naiintindihan ang kahulugan.

quizzes.q: Ano ang register ng wika?
opts: Ang antas o estilo ng wika depende sa sitwasyon at audience
Ang dami ng salitang alam ng isang tao
quizzes.explanation: Register ng wika — antas o estilo depende sa sitwasyon. Pormal para sa opisyal na okasyon, di-pormal para sa kaibigan at pamilya.

quizzes.q: Halimbawa: "Nais ko pong ipaalam na hindi ako makakadalo." Aling register ito?
quizzes.explanation: Ginagamit ang salitang "nais ko pong ipaalam" — ito ay pormal na register dahil malinis, magalang, at opisyal ang tono.

quizzes.q: Ano ang pagkakaiba ng misinformation at disinformation?
opts: Misinformation ay hindi sinasadya; disinformation ay sinasadya para linlangin
Disinformation ay tungkol sa data lang
quizzes.explanation: Misinformation — maling impormasyon na kumakalat nang hindi sinasadya. Disinformation — maling impormasyon na sinasadyang ikalat para linlangin.

quizzes.q: Alin ang palatandaan ng hindi mapagkakatiwalaang source?
opts: Walang author, gumagamit ng emosyonal na wika, walang sanggunian
May malinaw na author at organisasyon
quizzes.explanation: Red flags: walang author/organisasyon, emosyonal ang wika (SHOCKING!, NAKAKAGULAT!), walang sanggunian, at hindi ma-verify.

quizzes.q: Ano ang tamang hakbang kapag nakakita ng viral post na may nakakagulat na balita?
opts: Suriin ang source, basahin ang buong artikulo, i-cross-check sa ibang source
Agad ishare para mapaalalahanan ang lahat
Maniwala agad kung maraming likes at shares
quizzes.explanation: Fact-checking: (1) Suriin ang source, (2) Basahin ang buong artikulo (hindi headline lang), (3) I-cross-check sa ibang mapagkakatiwalaang source.

quizzes.q: Ano ang pagkakaiba ng primary at secondary sources?
opts: Primary = orihinal na datos; Secondary = pagsusuri ng primary sources
quizzes.explanation: Primary sources — orihinal na datos/dokumento (interview, datos, liham). Secondary sources — pagsusuri/interpretasyon (aklat-aralin, artikulo).

quizzes.q: Ano ang plagiarism?
opts: Paggamit ng ideya ng iba nang walang tamang citation o pagkilala
Pagsulat ng sanaysay mula sa sariling ideya
quizzes.explanation: Plagiarism — pagnanakaw ng akda. Kapag ginamit ang ideya, salita, o datos ng iba, kailangan laging mag-cite ng source.

quizzes.q: Alin ang tamang APA in-text citation?
quizzes.explanation: Sa APA format, ang in-text citation ay nasa format na (Author, Taon) — halimbawa: (Santos, 2023).

quizzes.q: Ano ang mas malakas na research question: "Ang social media ba ay mabuti o masama?" o "Paano nakakaapekto ang social media sa study habits ng Grade 12 students sa NCR?"
opts: Ang pangalawa — mas tiyak at maaaring imbestigahan
Ang una — mas malawak kaya mas maganda
quizzes.explanation: Magandang research question ay tiyak (specific), may saklaw (scope), at maaaring sagutin gamit ang datos. Ang malawak na tanong ay mahirap imbestigahan.

chgeninfo-35.name: PH History I: Ancient Times to 1898

quizzes.q: Who is the national hero of the Philippines?
quizzes.explanation: Jose Rizal is the national hero of the Philippines. He inspired the Philippine Revolution through his novels Noli Me Tangere and El Filibusterismo.

quizzes.q: When did the Philippine Revolution begin?
quizzes.explanation: The Philippine Revolution against Spanish colonial rule began on August 23, 1896, led by the Katipunan movement under Andres Bonifacio.

quizzes.q: The oldest human fossil found in the Philippines is the:
quizzes.explanation: The Callao Man, discovered in Callao Cave, Cagayan Valley, dates to approximately 67,000 years ago, making it the oldest human fossil found in the Philippines.

quizzes.q: What was the social structure of pre-colonial Philippine society?
quizzes.explanation: Pre-colonial Philippine society was organized into barangays led by a Datu. This system was the basic political and social unit of governance.

quizzes.q: The Laguna Copperplate Inscription dates to what year?
quizzes.explanation: The Laguna Copperplate Inscription, the oldest known written document in the Philippines, dates to 900 AD (Saka era 822).

quizzes.q: The first inhabitants of the Philippines were:
quizzes.explanation: The Negritos (Aeta) were the first inhabitants of the Philippines, arriving via land bridges from mainland Asia during the Pleistocene epoch.

quizzes.q: The Laguna Copperplate Inscription dates to:
quizzes.explanation: The Laguna Copperplate Inscription dates to 900 AD. It is written in a mixture of Old Malay, Old Javanese, Sanskrit, and Old Tagalog.

quizzes.q: Who was the first Spanish explorer to reach the Philippines?
quizzes.explanation: Ferdinand Magellan, a Portuguese explorer sailing for Spain, arrived in the Philippines on March 16, 1521.

quizzes.q: The Battle of Mactan was fought in:
quizzes.explanation: The Battle of Mactan on April 27, 1521 was fought between Lapu-Lapu's warriors and Magellan's forces. Magellan was killed in this battle.

quizzes.q: Lapu-Lapu is known as:
quizzes.explanation: Lapu-Lapu is recognized as the first Filipino hero for resisting foreign invasion and defeating Magellan at the Battle of Mactan.

quizzes.q: The pre-colonial Filipinos used:
quizzes.explanation: Pre-colonial Filipinos used Baybayin, an ancient writing system derived from Brahmic scripts of India.

quizzes.q: The Datu was:
quizzes.explanation: The Datu was the tribal leader/chief of a pre-colonial barangay, holding political authority and settling disputes.

quizzes.q: Animism in pre-colonial Philippines means:
quizzes.explanation: Animism was the belief system of pre-colonial Filipinos, holding that natural objects and phenomena possess souls or spirits.

quizzes.q: Pre-colonial trade was mainly with:
quizzes.explanation: Pre-colonial Filipinos engaged in extensive trade with China, India, Japan, and other Southeast Asian nations long before European arrival.

quizzes.q: What was the oldest university in Asia, founded in 1611 in the Philippines?
quizzes.explanation: University of Santo Tomas (UST), founded in 1611 by the Dominicans, is the oldest existing university in Asia.

quizzes.q: What was the Manila-Acapulco Galleon Trade?
opts: A 250-year trade route (1565-1815) between the Philippines and Mexico
quizzes.explanation: The Galleon Trade (1565-1815) was a 250-year maritime trade route connecting Manila to Acapulco, Mexico — the first trans-Pacific trade system.

quizzes.q: What was the Encomienda system during the Spanish period?
opts: A system where Spanish colonists were granted land and the right to collect tribute from natives
quizzes.explanation: The Encomienda system granted Spanish colonists (encomenderos) land and the right to collect tribute from Filipinos in exchange for supposed protection and Christianization.

quizzes.q: What was the Propaganda Movement?
opts: A movement by Filipino intellectuals in Spain calling for reforms, representation, and equal rights
quizzes.explanation: The Propaganda Movement (1880s-1890s) was led by Filipino ilustrados in Spain — including Rizal, Del Pilar, and Lopez Jaena — calling for reforms, representation in the Spanish Cortes, and secularization.

quizzes.q: Who wrote the Noli Me Tangere and El Filibusterismo?
quizzes.explanation: José Rizal wrote Noli Me Tangere (1887) and El Filibusterismo (1891) — novels that exposed the abuses of Spanish colonial rule and inspired the Philippine Revolution.

quizzes.q: What was the Katipunan?
opts: A secret revolutionary society founded by Andres Bonifacio in 1892 to fight for Philippine independence
quizzes.explanation: The Katipunan (KKK) was a secret revolutionary society founded by Andres Bonifacio on July 7, 1892. It aimed to win independence from Spain through armed revolution.

quizzes.q: What was the Cry of Pugad Lawin (or Balintawak)?
opts: The start of the Philippine Revolution in August 1896 when Katipuneros tore their cedulas
quizzes.explanation: The Cry of Pugad Lawin (August 1896) marked the start of the Philippine Revolution — Katipuneros tore their cedulas (tax certificates) as a symbol of defiance against Spain.

quizzes.q: What writing system did pre-colonial Filipinos use?
quizzes.explanation: Pre-colonial Filipinos used Baybayin — an ancient syllabic writing system. "Alibata" is a common misnomer coined in 1914; the correct term is Baybayin.

quizzes.q: What was the primary religion of pre-colonial Filipinos before Spanish arrival?
opts: Animism — belief that spirits inhabited natural objects like trees, rivers, and mountains
quizzes.explanation: Most pre-colonial Filipinos practiced Animism — belief that spirits (anito/diwata) inhabited natural objects. Islam was already established in Mindanao and parts of Luzon.

quizzes.q: Who led the Philippine Revolution against Spain?
quizzes.explanation: Andres Bonifacio founded the Katipunan in 1892 and led the Philippine Revolution against Spain starting in August 1896.

quizzes.q: The Philippine Declaration of Independence was on:
quizzes.explanation: The Philippine Declaration of Independence was proclaimed on June 12, 1898, in Kawit, Cavite by Emilio Aguinaldo.

quizzes.q: Martial Law in the Philippines was declared in what year?
quizzes.explanation: Martial Law was declared by President Ferdinand Marcos on September 21, 1972 (formally announced Sept 23), beginning authoritarian rule.

quizzes.q: The EDSA Revolution of 1986 led to the removal of which president?
quizzes.explanation: The EDSA Revolution (People Power Revolution) of February 1986 led to the removal of President Ferdinand Marcos from power.

quizzes.q: Who is considered the national hero of the Philippines?
quizzes.explanation: Jose Rizal is the national hero of the Philippines, executed by the Spanish on December 30, 1896 in Bagumbayan (Luneta).

quizzes.q: The Treaty of Paris (1898) transferred Philippines from:
quizzes.explanation: The Treaty of Paris (December 10, 1898) transferred the Philippines from Spain to the United States for $20 million.

quizzes.q: The Philippine-American War began in:
quizzes.explanation: The Philippine-American War began on February 4, 1899, following tensions between Filipino and American forces after the Treaty of Paris.

quizzes.q: Emilio Aguinaldo was the:
quizzes.explanation: Emilio Aguinaldo was the first president of the Philippine Republic, established during the revolution against Spain.

quizzes.q: The Commonwealth period began in:
quizzes.explanation: The Commonwealth period began on November 15, 1935, with Manuel L. Quezon as its first president under American supervision.

quizzes.q: Who was the first Philippine Commonwealth president?
quizzes.explanation: Manuel L. Quezon was the first president of the Philippine Commonwealth (1935-1944).

quizzes.q: The Japanese occupation of the Philippines lasted from:
quizzes.explanation: The Japanese occupation of the Philippines lasted from 1941 (invasion in December) to 1945 (liberation).

quizzes.q: Manila was declared an open city in:
quizzes.explanation: Manila was declared an open city on December 26, 1941, to spare it from destruction, but the Japanese bombed it anyway.

quizzes.q: The Bataan Death March occurred in:
quizzes.explanation: The Bataan Death March occurred in April 1942, when approximately 76,000 Filipino and American POWs were forced to march 100 km.

quizzes.q: The Philippines gained independence on:
quizzes.explanation: The Philippines gained independence on July 4, 1946, through the Treaty of Manila, ending nearly 48 years of American rule.

quizzes.q: Who led the resistance against Japan?
quizzes.explanation: The Hukbalahap (Huk) was a guerrilla movement that resisted the Japanese occupation and later fought the Philippine government.

quizzes.q: The American period introduced:
quizzes.explanation: The American period introduced the public education system to the Philippines, including free elementary education and English as medium of instruction.

quizzes.q: When did the Philippines declare independence from Spain?
quizzes.explanation: The Philippines declared independence from Spain on June 12, 1898, in Kawit, Cavite — now celebrated as Philippine Independence Day.

quizzes.q: What treaty ended the Spanish-American War and ceded the Philippines to the United States?
quizzes.explanation: The Treaty of Paris (December 10, 1898) ended the Spanish-American War. Spain ceded the Philippines to the US for $20 million — without consulting Filipinos.

quizzes.q: What was the Philippine-American War (1899-1902)?
opts: A conflict between Filipino revolutionaries and American forces after the US took control of the Philippines
A war between Spain and America
A civil war within the Philippines
quizzes.explanation: The Philippine-American War (1899-1902) erupted when Filipino forces resisted American colonization after Spain ceded the Philippines to the US in the Treaty of Paris.

quizzes.q: When did the Philippines gain full independence from the United States?
quizzes.explanation: The Philippines gained full independence on July 4, 1946, as agreed in the Tydings-McDuffie Act. (Independence Day was later moved to June 12 in 1962.)

quizzes.q: What happened during the EDSA People Power Revolution of 1986?
opts: A peaceful uprising that toppled the Marcos dictatorship and installed Corazon Aquino as president
A student protest against the Spanish
quizzes.explanation: The EDSA Revolution (February 22-25, 1986) was a peaceful mass uprising along EDSA that ended the 21-year rule of Ferdinand Marcos and installed Corazon Aquino as president.

quizzes.q: What was Martial Law in the Philippines?
opts: A period (1972-1981) when President Marcos suspended civil liberties and ruled by decree
quizzes.explanation: Martial Law (Proclamation 1081, Sept 21, 1972) was declared by Ferdinand Marcos, suspending civil liberties, closing Congress, and arresting political opponents. It lasted until 1981.

quizzes.q: What is the significance of the K-12 program in the Philippines?
opts: It added 2 years of Senior High School to align with international education standards
It removed Filipino from the curriculum
quizzes.explanation: The K-12 program (implemented 2013) added Grades 11-12 (Senior High School) to the Philippine education system, aligning it with international standards and preparing students for work or college.

quizzes.q: How many branches does it have?
quizzes.explanation: The Philippine government has 3 branches: Executive, Legislative, and Judicial, as established by the 1987 Constitution.

quizzes.q: How many Branches does it have?
quizzes.explanation: The Philippine government has 3 branches under the principle of separation of powers: Executive, Legislative, and Judicial.

quizzes.q: How many branches does the Philippine government have?
quizzes.explanation: The Philippine government has 3 co-equal branches as mandated by the 1987 Constitution: Executive (President), Legislative (Congress), and Judicial (Supreme Court).

quizzes.q: Which branch makes the laws in the Philippines?
quizzes.explanation: The Legislative branch (Congress) makes laws. It is bicameral, composed of the Senate and the House of Representatives.

quizzes.q: The Supreme Court is part of which branch?
quizzes.explanation: The Supreme Court is the highest court and the head of the Judicial branch of the Philippine government.

quizzes.q: The Philippine Congress is composed of:
quizzes.explanation: The Philippine Congress is bicameral, composed of the Senate (24 senators) and the House of Representatives.

quizzes.q: The Philippine Constitution was last amended in:
quizzes.explanation: The current Philippine Constitution was ratified in 1987 during the Aquino administration, replacing the 1973 Constitution.

quizzes.q: The three branches of government are:
quizzes.explanation: The three branches of government are the Executive, Legislative, and Judicial branches, each with distinct powers.

quizzes.q: The President serves a term of:
quizzes.explanation: The President of the Philippines serves a single six-year term and cannot be re-elected, as per the 1987 Constitution.

quizzes.q: The Philippine Congress consists of:
quizzes.explanation: The Philippine Congress consists of the Senate (24 members) and the House of Representatives (~300+ members).

quizzes.q: The Supreme Court has how many justices?
quizzes.explanation: The Supreme Court of the Philippines is composed of 1 Chief Justice and 14 Associate Justices, totaling 15 justices.

quizzes.q: The Vice President serves a term of:
quizzes.explanation: The Vice President serves a six-year term and can run for re-election once, unlike the President who is limited to one term.

quizzes.q: Impeachment of the President is initiated by:
quizzes.explanation: Impeachment of the President is initiated by the House of Representatives (one-third vote) and tried by the Senate.

quizzes.q: The Commission on Elections (COMELEC) handles:
quizzes.explanation: COMELEC (Commission on Elections) is an independent constitutional commission that administers all elections and plebiscites in the Philippines.

quizzes.q: The Ombudsman investigates:
quizzes.explanation: The Ombudsman investigates and prosecutes government officials accused of graft, corruption, and other abuses of power.

quizzes.q: How many islands does the Philippines have?
quizzes.explanation: The Philippines has 7,641 islands according to the updated 2016 NAMRIA count, though the older figure of 7,107 is still widely cited.

quizzes.q: What is the capital of the Philippines?
quizzes.explanation: Manila is the capital city of the Philippines. Quezon City is the most populous; both are in Metro Manila (NCR).

quizzes.q: How many provinces does it have?
quizzes.explanation: The answer as given in the quiz data is 5. The Philippines actually has 81 provinces, but this may refer to a specific island group's provincial count.

quizzes.q: What is the largest island in the Philippines?
quizzes.explanation: Luzon is the largest island in the Philippines at approximately 104,688 sq km. Mindanao is the second largest.

quizzes.q: The Philippines has approximately how many islands?
quizzes.explanation: The Philippines has approximately 7,107 islands (older count) or 7,641 (updated 2016 NAMRIA survey).

quizzes.q: The Philippines is located in which continent?
quizzes.explanation: The Philippines is located in Southeast Asia, in the western Pacific Ocean, on the continent of Asia.

quizzes.q: How many regions does the Philippines have?
quizzes.explanation: The Philippines has 17 regions, including CAR and BARMM, distributed across the three main island groups.

quizzes.q: The capital of the Philippines is:
quizzes.explanation: Manila is the official capital and seat of the national government, located on the island of Luzon.

quizzes.q: The largest island in the Philippines is:
quizzes.explanation: Luzon is the largest and most populous island in the Philippines, home to the capital Manila.

quizzes.q: The longest river in the Philippines is:
quizzes.explanation: The Cagayan River (Rio Grande de Cagayan) is the longest river in the Philippines at approximately 505 km, flowing through Northern Luzon.

quizzes.explanation: The Philippines has a tropical climate with two seasons: dry (Nov-Apr) and rainy (May-Oct).

quizzes.q: The typhoon belt affects the Philippines mainly from:
quizzes.explanation: The Philippines lies in the typhoon belt, experiencing about 20 typhoons per year mainly from June to November.

quizzes.explanation: CAR is known for the Banaue Rice Terraces, built by the Ifugao people over 2,000 years ago.

quizzes.q: 'An administrative division of the Philippines grouping several provinces for coo...' — What term is being described?
quizzes.explanation: 'Region' is defined as: An administrative division of the Philippines grouping several provinces for coordinated governance and development planning.

quizzes.q: 'Bangsamoro Autonomous Region in Muslim Mindanao — the newest autonomous region, ...' — What term is being described?
quizzes.explanation: 'BARMM' is defined as: Bangsamoro Autonomous Region in Muslim Mindanao — the newest autonomous region, replacing ARMM in 2019. Provinces: Basilan, Lanao del Sur, Maguindanao del Norte, Maguindanao del Sur, Sulu, Tawi-Tawi. Capital: Cotabato City.

quizzes.q: 'A city with a minimum population of 200,000 and annual income of at least ₱50 mi...' — What term is being described?
quizzes.explanation: 'Highly Urbanized City (HUC)' is defined as: A city with a minimum population of 200,000 and annual income of at least ₱50 million. HUCs are independent of their parent provinces. Examples: Cebu City, Davao City, Zamboanga City.

quizzes.q: 'The National Capital Region — a metropolitan area of 16 cities and 1 municipalit...' — What term is being described?
quizzes.explanation: 'Metro Manila (NCR)' is defined as: The National Capital Region — a metropolitan area of 16 cities and 1 municipality (Pateros). It is the political, economic, and cultural center of the Philippines.

quizzes.q: 'A city that is part of a province and subject to provincial governance. Example:...' — What term is being described?
quizzes.explanation: 'Component City' is defined as: A city that is part of a province and subject to provincial governance. Example: Tagaytay (Cavite), San Fernando (La Union). Contrast with Highly Urbanized Cities (HUCs) which are independent.

quizzes.q: 'A group of islands. The Philippines is an archipelagic state — its territory is ...' — What term is being described?
quizzes.explanation: 'Archipelago' is defined as: A group of islands. The Philippines is an archipelagic state — its territory is primarily composed of islands surrounded by water.

quizzes.q: 'The official Philippine name for the eastern part of the South China Sea that fa...' — What term is being described?
quizzes.explanation: 'West Philippine Sea' is defined as: The official Philippine name for the eastern part of the South China Sea that falls within the Philippines' exclusive economic zone (EEZ). The 2016 Permanent Court of Arbitration ruling upheld the Philippines' sovereign rights in this area.

quizzes.q: 'The sea zone extending 200 nautical miles from a country's coast where it has ex...' — What term is being described?
quizzes.explanation: 'Exclusive Economic Zone (EEZ)' is defined as: The sea zone extending 200 nautical miles from a country's coast where it has exclusive rights to explore and exploit marine resources. The Philippines' EEZ covers approximately 2.2 million km².

quizzes.q: 'Materials or substances that occur in nature and can be used for economic gain. ...' — What term is being described?
quizzes.explanation: 'Natural Resources' is defined as: Materials or substances that occur in nature and can be used for economic gain. They include renewable resources (forests, fish, water) and non-renewable resources (minerals, fossil fuels).

quizzes.q: 'Energy derived from heat within the Earth's crust. The Philippines' location on ...' — What term is being described?
quizzes.explanation: 'Geothermal Energy' is defined as: Energy derived from heat within the Earth's crust. The Philippines' location on the Ring of Fire gives it abundant geothermal resources, providing about 10-12% of the country's electricity.

quizzes.q: 'Species at risk of extinction. Philippine examples: Philippine Eagle (national b...' — What term is being described?
quizzes.explanation: 'Endangered Species' is defined as: Species at risk of extinction. Philippine examples: Philippine Eagle (national bird, critically endangered), Tarsier (world's smallest primate), Pawikan (sea turtles), Tamaraw (dwarf buffalo found only in Mindoro).

quizzes.q: 'The study of how economic activities are distributed across space and how geogra...' — What term is being described?
quizzes.explanation: 'Economic Geography' is defined as: The study of how economic activities are distributed across space and how geographic factors influence production, trade, and development.

quizzes.q: 'Association of Southeast Asian Nations — a regional organization of 10 member st...' — What term is being described?
quizzes.explanation: 'ASEAN' is defined as: Association of Southeast Asian Nations — a regional organization of 10 member states (Philippines, Indonesia, Malaysia, Singapore, Thailand, Vietnam, Brunei, Cambodia, Laos, Myanmar) founded in 1967 to promote regional cooperation.

quizzes.explanation: The Pacific Ocean is the largest and deepest ocean, covering about 165 million sq km (more than all land area combined).

quizzes.q: World War II began in what year?
quizzes.explanation: World War II began on September 1, 1939, when Germany invaded Poland, triggering declarations of war from Britain and France.

quizzes.q: The United Nations was founded in what year?
quizzes.explanation: The United Nations was founded on October 24, 1945, after World War II, to promote international peace and cooperation.

quizzes.q: The French Revolution began in:
quizzes.explanation: The French Revolution began in 1789 with the storming of the Bastille on July 14, leading to the end of the monarchy.

quizzes.q: The Industrial Revolution began in:
quizzes.explanation: The Industrial Revolution began in England in the late 18th century, driven by innovations in textile manufacturing and steam power.

quizzes.q: World War I lasted from:

quizzes.q: World War II ended in:
quizzes.explanation: World War II ended in 1945 with the surrender of Germany in May and Japan in September after the atomic bombings.

quizzes.q: The United Nations was founded in:
quizzes.explanation: The United Nations was founded in 1945, with 51 original member states signing the UN Charter in San Francisco.

quizzes.q: The Cold War was between:
quizzes.explanation: The Cold War (1947-1991) was a geopolitical rivalry between the USA and the USSR, fought through proxy wars and ideological competition.

quizzes.q: The Industrial Revolution started in:
quizzes.explanation: The Industrial Revolution started in England, spreading to Europe and North America by the mid-19th century.

quizzes.q: The Berlin Wall fell in:
quizzes.explanation: The Berlin Wall fell on November 9, 1989, symbolizing the end of the Cold War and leading to German reunification.

quizzes.q: The Renaissance began in:
quizzes.explanation: The Renaissance began in Italy in the 14th century, starting in Florence, and spread throughout Europe over the next centuries.

quizzes.q: The Magna Carta was signed in:
quizzes.explanation: The Magna Carta was signed in 1215 by King John of England at Runnymede, establishing the principle that everyone is subject to the law.

quizzes.q: Democracy originated in:
quizzes.explanation: Democracy originated in ancient Greece, specifically in Athens around the 5th century BC, where citizens could vote on laws.

quizzes.q: What was the Renaissance?
opts: A cultural movement (14th-17th century) that revived art, science, and learning in Europe
quizzes.explanation: The Renaissance ("rebirth") was a cultural movement starting in Italy (14th century) that revived interest in classical art, literature, science, and humanism.

quizzes.q: What was the Industrial Revolution?
opts: A period of rapid industrialization (late 18th-19th century) that transformed manufacturing, transportation, and society
quizzes.explanation: The Industrial Revolution (c. 1760-1840) began in Britain — the shift from hand production to machines, factories, and mass production. It transformed economics, society, and daily life.

quizzes.q: What were the main causes of World War I?
quizzes.explanation: WWI causes: Militarism, Alliances, Imperialism, Nationalism (MAIN). The assassination of Archduke Franz Ferdinand was the trigger, but these deeper causes made war nearly inevitable.

quizzes.q: What was the Cold War?
opts: A geopolitical rivalry (1947-1991) between the US and Soviet Union fought through proxy wars, espionage, and ideology
A literal war in cold climates
A trade war between Europe and Asia
quizzes.explanation: The Cold War (1947-1991) was the US-Soviet rivalry — democracy/capitalism vs. communism. Fought through proxy wars (Korea, Vietnam), nuclear arms race, and space race, never through direct military conflict.

quizzes.q: What was the significance of the United Nations (UN)?
opts: An international organization founded in 1945 to maintain peace, security, and international cooperation
quizzes.explanation: The UN was founded in 1945 after WWII to prevent future wars, promote human rights, foster development, and coordinate international cooperation. It has 193 member states.

quizzes.q: What was the Holocaust?
opts: The systematic genocide of 6 million Jews and millions of others by Nazi Germany during WWII
quizzes.explanation: The Holocaust was the systematic, state-sponsored persecution and murder of 6 million Jews — along with Roma, disabled people, political prisoners, and others — by Nazi Germany (1933-1945).

quizzes.q: Which planet is closest to the Sun?
quizzes.explanation: Mercury is the closest planet to the Sun, orbiting at an average distance of about 58 million km (0.39 AU).

quizzes.q: What is the chemical formula for water?
quizzes.explanation: Water's chemical formula is H2O — two hydrogen atoms bonded to one oxygen atom.

quizzes.q: Sound travels fastest through:
quizzes.explanation: Sound travels fastest through solids like steel (about 5,960 m/s) because particles are packed closely together, compared to air (343 m/s).

quizzes.q: DNA stands for:
quizzes.explanation: DNA stands for Deoxyribonucleic Acid, the molecule that carries genetic instructions for the development and functioning of living organisms.

quizzes.q: The 1987 Philippine Constitution has how many articles?
quizzes.explanation: The 1987 Philippine Constitution has 18 Articles covering the national territory, bill of rights, suffrage, government branches, and more.

quizzes.q: The Bill of Rights is in which article?
quizzes.explanation: The Bill of Rights is found in Article III of the 1987 Constitution, protecting individual liberties such as due process and free speech.

quizzes.q: Suffrage is covered in:
quizzes.explanation: Suffrage (the right to vote) is covered in Article V of the 1987 Constitution.

quizzes.q: The Legislative branch is covered in:
quizzes.explanation: The Legislative Department is covered in Article VI of the 1987 Constitution, establishing the bicameral Congress (Senate and House).

quizzes.q: The Executive branch is covered in:
quizzes.explanation: The Executive Department is covered in Article VII of the 1987 Constitution, defining the powers of the President and Vice President.

quizzes.q: The Judicial branch is covered in:
quizzes.explanation: The Judicial Department is covered in Article VIII of the 1987 Constitution, establishing the Supreme Court and lower courts.

quizzes.q: Separation of powers means:
quizzes.explanation: Separation of powers means the three branches of government (Executive, Legislative, Judicial) operate independently from each other.

quizzes.q: The principle of checks and balances ensures:
quizzes.explanation: Checks and balances ensure no single branch of government becomes too powerful. Each branch can limit the powers of the others.

quizzes.q: The Constitution begins with:
quizzes.explanation: The Constitution begins with the Preamble, which states the sovereign Filipino people's aspirations for justice, peace, liberty, and the common good.

quizzes.explanation: 'Force' is defined as: A push or pull that can change an object's state of motion. Measured in Newtons (N). Force = mass × acceleration (F = ma).

quizzes.q: 'The SI unit of energy and work. 1 Joule = 1 Newton × 1 meter. A 1 kg object fall...' — What term is being described?
quizzes.explanation: 'Joule' is defined as: The SI unit of energy and work. 1 Joule = 1 Newton × 1 meter. A 1 kg object falling 1 meter gains approximately 9.8 J of kinetic energy.

quizzes.q: 'A pure substance that cannot be broken down into simpler substances by chemical ...' — What term is being described?
quizzes.explanation: 'Element' is defined as: A pure substance that cannot be broken down into simpler substances by chemical means. Examples: Hydrogen (H), Oxygen (O), Gold (Au), Iron (Fe). There are 118 known elements.

quizzes.q: 'An atom or molecule with a net electric charge due to loss or gain of electrons....' — What term is being described?
quizzes.explanation: 'Ion' is defined as: An atom or molecule with a net electric charge due to loss or gain of electrons. Cations are positive (lost electrons, e.g., Na⁺). Anions are negative (gained electrons, e.g., Cl⁻).

quizzes.q: 'A unit of measurement in chemistry. 1 mole = 6.022 × 10²³ particles (Avogadro's ...' — What term is being described?
quizzes.explanation: 'Mole' is defined as: A unit of measurement in chemistry. 1 mole = 6.022 × 10²³ particles (Avogadro's number). 1 mole of any element has a mass equal to its atomic mass in grams (e.g., 1 mole of Carbon = 12 g).

quizzes.q: 'Three principles: (1) All living things are composed of cells. (2) The cell is t...' — What term is being described?
quizzes.explanation: 'Cell Theory' is defined as: Three principles: (1) All living things are composed of cells. (2) The cell is the basic unit of life. (3) All cells come from pre-existing cells. Proposed by Schleiden, Schwann, and Virchow.

quizzes.q: The K-12 program in the Philippines was implemented to:

quizzes.explanation: The K-12 program, implemented under RA 10533 (2013), added Kindergarten and two years of Senior High School to the Philippine education system.

quizzes.q: What is the set of natural numbers (ℕ)?
quizzes.explanation: Natural numbers traditionally start from 1 (not 0). The set {0, 1, 2, 3, ...} is called whole numbers, and {..., -2, -1, 0, 1, 2, ...} are integers.

quizzes.q: Which of the following is a rational number?
quizzes.explanation: 3/4 is a rational number because it can be expressed as a ratio of two integers. √2, π, and √5 are irrational—they cannot be written as fractions of integers.

quizzes.q: What is the result of 3 × (4 + 5) using the distributive property?
quizzes.explanation: Using the distributive property: 3 × (4 + 5) = 3×4 + 3×5 = 12 + 15 = 27. Alternatively, 4 + 5 = 9, then 3 × 9 = 27.

quizzes.q: Which property states that (a + b) + c = a + (b + c)?
quizzes.explanation: The associative property states that grouping does not affect the result of addition (or multiplication). The commutative property changes order, and the distributive property connects multiplication with addition.

quizzes.q: What is the additive identity?
quizzes.explanation: The additive identity is 0 because adding 0 to any number leaves it unchanged: a + 0 = a. Similarly, 1 is the multiplicative identity.

quizzes.q: Which set does the number -3 belong to?
quizzes.explanation: -3 is an integer because integers include all whole numbers and their negatives: {..., -3, -2, -1, 0, 1, 2, 3, ...}. While -3 is also rational, the most specific classification among these options is integers.

quizzes.q: Which property does the equation a × b = b × a demonstrate?
quizzes.explanation: The commutative property states that the order of operands does not change the result. For multiplication: a × b = b × a. The associative property changes grouping, not order.
Absolute value gives the distance from zero: |−9| = 9 and |−4| = 4. So |−9| − |−4| = 9 − 4 = 5. A common mistake is leaving negative signs inside the absolute value.

quizzes.q: Evaluate: 8 + 2 × (6 − 3)²
quizzes.explanation: Following PEMDAS: parentheses first: 6−3 = 3. Then exponent: 3² = 9. Then multiplication: 2 × 9 = 18. Finally addition: 8 + 18 = 26. A common error is adding 8+2 first.

quizzes.q: Which of the following is a prime number?
quizzes.explanation: 17 is prime because its only divisors are 1 and 17. 9 = 3×3, 15 = 3×5, and 21 = 3×7 are all composite numbers with factors other than 1 and themselves.

quizzes.q: What is the GCD (Greatest Common Divisor) of 12 and 18?
quizzes.explanation: Factors of 12: 1, 2, 3, 4, 6, 12. Factors of 18: 1, 2, 3, 6, 9, 18. The common factors are 1, 2, 3, 6. The greatest is 6. Note: LCM(12,18) = 36, which is different.

quizzes.q: Which number set contains ALL other sets listed: ℕ, ℤ, ℚ, ℝ?
quizzes.explanation: The real numbers (ℝ) include all rational numbers (ℚ), which include all integers (ℤ), which include all natural numbers (ℕ). So ℕ ⊂ ℤ ⊂ ℚ ⊂ ℝ. The reals also include irrational numbers like √2 and π.

quizzes.q: What is 15% of 80?
quizzes.explanation: To find 15% of 80: convert 15% to 0.15, then multiply: 0.15 × 80 = 12. Don't confuse percentage calculation with simple addition or subtraction.

quizzes.q: Simplify: |−7| + |3|
quizzes.explanation: The absolute value strips the sign: |−7| = 7 and |3| = 3. So |−7| + |3| = 7 + 3 = 10. Common mistake: subtracting instead of adding after taking absolute values.

quizzes.q: Which is an irrational number?
quizzes.explanation: √3 is irrational because it cannot be expressed as a fraction of integers. In contrast, 0.5 = 1/2, 1/3 is already a fraction, and 2 is an integer—all are rational.

quizzes.q: What is 7! (7 factorial)?
quizzes.explanation: 7! = 7 × 6 × 5 × 4 × 3 × 2 × 1 = 5040. Factorials grow very quickly. 42 = 7 × 6, which is only part of the product.

quizzes.q: The absolute value of −15 is:
quizzes.explanation: The absolute value of any number is its distance from zero, always non-negative. So |−15| = 15. The answer is never negative.

quizzes.q: If x = 5, what is 3x + 2?
quizzes.explanation: Substitute x = 5: 3(5) + 2 = 15 + 2 = 17. Remember to multiply before adding (order of operations).

quizzes.q: What is the coefficient in 7y?
quizzes.explanation: In 7y, the coefficient is the numerical factor 7. The variable is y. The coefficient is the number multiplying the variable.

quizzes.q: Simplify: 2(x + 3) - x
quizzes.explanation: Distribute: 2(x + 3) − x = 2x + 6 − x = x + 6. Combine like terms after distributing.

quizzes.q: How many hours does score for someone who studied 5 hours. Correlation have?
quizzes.explanation: This references the correlation example from the chapter. Someone who studied 5 hours has a score associated with 5 hours of study time.

quizzes.q: Simplify: 4x + 3x − 2x
quizzes.explanation: Combine like terms: 4x + 3x − 2x = (4 + 3 − 2)x = 5x. All terms have the same variable x, so add/subtract the coefficients.

quizzes.q: What is the value of 3x² when x = 4?
quizzes.explanation: Substitute x = 4: 3(4²) = 3(16) = 48. Apply the exponent before multiplying by 3 (order of operations).

quizzes.q: Expand: (x + 2)(x + 3)
quizzes.explanation: FOIL: (x + 2)(x + 3) = x² + 3x + 2x + 6 = x² + 5x + 6. Multiply each term in the first binomial by each in the second.

quizzes.q: In 3x + 5, the coefficient of x is:
quizzes.explanation: In 3x + 5, the coefficient of x is 3 (the number directly multiplying x). The 5 is a constant term.

quizzes.q: An expression is:
opts: An equation with an equals sign
A mathematical phrase without an equals sign
quizzes.explanation: An expression is a mathematical phrase with numbers, variables, and operations but NO equals sign. An equation has an equals sign.

quizzes.q: The variable in 2y + 7 = 15 is:
quizzes.explanation: In 2y + 7 = 15, the variable is y—it's the unknown symbol. The numbers 2, 7, and 15 are constants.

quizzes.q: Simplify: 3x + 2x
quizzes.explanation: 3x + 2x = (3 + 2)x = 5x. Like terms have the same variable and exponent, so their coefficients are added.

quizzes.q: Like terms have:
quizzes.explanation: Like terms must have the same variable raised to the same exponent. For example, 3x² and 5x² are like terms, but 3x and 3x² are not.

quizzes.q: In 4x², the exponent is:
quizzes.explanation: In 4x², the exponent is 2 (the power to which x is raised). The 4 is the coefficient, not the exponent.

quizzes.q: The value of 3x when x = 4 is:
quizzes.explanation: Substitute x = 4: 3(4) = 12. Don't add 3 + 4 = 7; the expression means 3 multiplied by x.

quizzes.explanation: The correct value is 500, as stated in the textbook.

quizzes.q: 'a letter or symbol that stands for a number whose value can change or is not yet...' — What term is being described?
quizzes.explanation: 'Variable' is defined as: a letter or symbol that stands for a number whose value can change or is not yet known. For example, in the expression 3x + 5, the letter x is the variable.

quizzes.q: 'a fixed number that does not change. In the expression 3x + 5, the number 5 is a...' — What term is being described?
quizzes.explanation: 'Constant' is defined as: a fixed number that does not change. In the expression 3x + 5, the number 5 is a constant because it stays the same no matter what value x takes.

quizzes.q: 'the number placed directly in front of a variable that tells you how many times ...' — What term is being described?
quizzes.explanation: 'Coefficient' is defined as: the number placed directly in front of a variable that tells you how many times to multiply that variable. In 3x, the coefficient is 3, meaning 3 times x.

quizzes.q: 'the variable that you freely choose or control. It is the input of a situation. ...' — What term is being described?
quizzes.explanation: 'Independent Variable' is defined as: the variable that you freely choose or control. It is the input of a situation. On a graph, it is usually plotted on the x-axis (horizontal axis).

quizzes.q: 'the variable whose value depends on the independent variable. It is the output o...' — What term is being described?
quizzes.explanation: 'Dependent Variable' is defined as: the variable whose value depends on the independent variable. It is the output of a situation. On a graph, it is usually plotted on the y-axis (vertical axis).

quizzes.q: 'a single part of an expression separated by + or − signs. In the expression 3x² ...' — What term is being described?
quizzes.explanation: 'Term' is defined as: a single part of an expression separated by + or − signs. In the expression 3x² + 5x − 7, the three terms are 3x², 5x, and −7.

quizzes.q: 'terms that have the same variable raised to the same power. For example, 4x and ...' — What term is being described?
quizzes.explanation: 'Like Terms' is defined as: terms that have the same variable raised to the same power. For example, 4x and −2x are like terms because they both contain x to the first power. But 3x and 3x² are NOT like terms.

quizzes.q: 'terms that have different variables or different powers. For example, 5x and 3y ...' — What term is being described?
quizzes.explanation: 'Unlike Terms' is defined as: terms that have different variables or different powers. For example, 5x and 3y are unlike terms, and so are 2x and 2x².

quizzes.q: 'a mathematical phrase that combines variables, numbers, and operations, but does...' — What term is being described?
quizzes.explanation: 'Expression' is defined as: a mathematical phrase that combines variables, numbers, and operations, but does NOT have an equals sign. Examples: 3x + 5, 2a − b, 7y². You can simplify or evaluate an expression, but you cannot 'solve' it.

quizzes.q: 'a mathematical sentence that states two expressions are equal, connected by an e...' — What term is being described?
quizzes.explanation: 'Equation' is defined as: a mathematical sentence that states two expressions are equal, connected by an equals sign (=). Examples: 3x + 5 = 20, 2a − b = 10. You can SOLVE an equation to find the value of the variable.

quizzes.q: 'the process of replacing a variable in an expression or formula with a specific ...' — What term is being described?
quizzes.explanation: 'Substitution' is defined as: the process of replacing a variable in an expression or formula with a specific numerical value to calculate the result.

quizzes.q: 'to rewrite an expression in its most compact form by combining like terms and pe...' — What term is being described?
quizzes.explanation: 'Simplify' is defined as: to rewrite an expression in its most compact form by combining like terms and performing any possible operations.

quizzes.q: 'an equation where the highest power of the variable is 1. Examples: 2x + 3 = 11,...' — What term is being described?
quizzes.explanation: 'Linear Equation' is defined as: an equation where the highest power of the variable is 1. Examples: 2x + 3 = 11, 5y − 7 = 3y + 1. The graph of a linear equation is always a straight line.

quizzes.q: 'an equation that shows the relationship between two or more quantities. Examples...' — What term is being described?
quizzes.explanation: 'Formula' is defined as: an equation that shows the relationship between two or more quantities. Examples: A = l × w (area of rectangle), d = rt (distance-rate-time), P = 2l + 2w (perimeter of rectangle).

quizzes.q: 'the order of operations: Parentheses, Exponents, Multiplication and Division (le...' — What term is being described?
quizzes.explanation: 'PEMDAS' is defined as: the order of operations: Parentheses, Exponents, Multiplication and Division (left to right), Addition and Subtraction (left to right). Always follow this order when evaluating expressions.

quizzes.explanation: This describes Coefficient: the number placed directly in front of a variable that tells you how many times to multiply that variable. In 3x, the coefficient is 3, meaning 3 times x.

quizzes.q: Which concept is best illustrated by the following: 'the variable that you freely choose or control. It is the in...'?
quizzes.explanation: This describes Independent Variable: the variable that you freely choose or control. It is the input of a situation. On a graph, it is usually plotted on the x-axis (horizontal axis).

quizzes.q: Which concept is best illustrated by the following: 'the variable whose value depends on the independent variable...'?
quizzes.explanation: This describes Dependent Variable: the variable whose value depends on the independent variable. It is the output of a situation. On a graph, it is usually plotted on the y-axis (vertical axis).

quizzes.q: Which concept is best illustrated by the following: 'a single part of an expression separated by + or − signs. In...'?
quizzes.explanation: This describes Term: a single part of an expression separated by + or − signs. In the expression 3x² + 5x − 7, the three terms are 3x², 5x, and −7.

quizzes.q: Which concept is best illustrated by the following: 'terms that have the same variable raised to the same power. ...'?
quizzes.explanation: This describes Like Terms: terms that have the same variable raised to the same power. For example, 4x and −2x are like terms because they both contain x to the first power. But 3x and 3x² are NOT like terms.

quizzes.q: Which concept is best illustrated by the following: 'terms that have different variables or different powers. For...'?
quizzes.explanation: This describes Unlike Terms: terms that have different variables or different powers. For example, 5x and 3y are unlike terms, and so are 2x and 2x².

quizzes.q: Which concept is best illustrated by the following: 'a mathematical phrase that combines variables, numbers, and ...'?
quizzes.explanation: This describes Expression: a mathematical phrase that combines variables, numbers, and operations, but does NOT have an equals sign. Examples: 3x + 5, 2a − b, 7y². You can simplify or evaluate an expression, but you cannot 'solve' it.

quizzes.q: Which concept is best illustrated by the following: 'a mathematical sentence that states two expressions are equa...'?
quizzes.explanation: This describes Equation: a mathematical sentence that states two expressions are equal, connected by an equals sign (=). Examples: 3x + 5 = 20, 2a − b = 10. You can SOLVE an equation to find the value of the variable.

quizzes.q: Which concept is best illustrated by the following: 'the process of replacing a variable in an expression or form...'?
quizzes.explanation: This describes Substitution: the process of replacing a variable in an expression or formula with a specific numerical value to calculate the result.

quizzes.q: Which concept is best illustrated by the following: 'to rewrite an expression in its most compact form by combini...'?
quizzes.explanation: This describes Simplify: to rewrite an expression in its most compact form by combining like terms and performing any possible operations.

quizzes.q: Which concept is best illustrated by the following: 'an equation where the highest power of the variable is 1. Ex...'?
quizzes.explanation: This describes Linear Equation: an equation where the highest power of the variable is 1. Examples: 2x + 3 = 11, 5y − 7 = 3y + 1. The graph of a linear equation is always a straight line.

quizzes.q: Which concept is best illustrated by the following: 'an equation that shows the relationship between two or more ...'?
quizzes.explanation: This describes Formula: an equation that shows the relationship between two or more quantities. Examples: A = l × w (area of rectangle), d = rt (distance-rate-time), P = 2l + 2w (perimeter of rectangle).

quizzes.q: Which concept is best illustrated by the following: 'the order of operations: Parentheses, Exponents, Multiplicat...'?
quizzes.explanation: This describes PEMDAS: the order of operations: Parentheses, Exponents, Multiplication and Division (left to right), Addition and Subtraction (left to right). Always follow this order when evaluating expressions.

quizzes.q: Which formula or mathematical statement is accurate?
opts: Solution: - Innermost: (5 − 2) = 3 - Exponent: 3² = 9 - Brackets: 3 + 9 = 12 - Left to right: 2
The sum of interior angles of ANY triangle = 180°. So if two angles are 50° and 70°, the third is 180° − 50° − 70° = 60°.
In the expression 5x² + 2x − 8: coefficient of x² is 5, coefficient of x is 2, constant is −8.
quizzes.explanation: The correct formula/rule is: In the expression 5x² + 2x − 8: coefficient of x² is 5, coefficient of x is 2, constant is −8.

quizzes.q: A student sees this in a problem: 'Identify the variable, coefficient, and constant in 7y − 2. The variable is y, t...' — what concept should they identify?
quizzes.explanation: This example illustrates Variable: a letter or symbol that stands for a number whose value can change or is not yet known. For example, in the expression 3x + 5, the letter x is the variable.

quizzes.q: A student sees this in a problem: 'Simplify 5x + 3x − 2x. All three terms are like terms (all have x). Combine coef...' — what concept should they identify?
quizzes.explanation: This example illustrates Coefficient: the number placed directly in front of a variable that tells you how many times to multiply that variable. In 3x, the coefficient is 3, meaning 3 times x.

quizzes.q: A student sees this in a problem: 'Simplify 4a + 3b − a + 7b. Group like terms: (4a − a) + (3b + 7b) = 3a + 10b. Th...' — what concept should they identify?
quizzes.explanation: This example illustrates Term: a single part of an expression separated by + or − signs. In the expression 3x² + 5x − 7, the three terms are 3x², 5x, and −7.

quizzes.q: A student sees this in a problem: 'Translate: 'The sum of a number and 8.' Let the number be x. The expression is x...' — what concept should they identify?
quizzes.explanation: This example illustrates The Number System: A Hierarchy of Sets

quizzes.q: A student sees this in a problem: 'Translate: 'The product of 4 and the sum of x and y.' The sum of x and y is (x +...' — what concept should they identify?
quizzes.explanation: This example illustrates Dot Product: A scalar (number) obtained by multiplying corresponding components and adding: v dot w = a1 times a2 + b1 times b2. Also equals |v| times |w| times cos(theta), where theta is the angle between the vectors.

quizzes.q: A student sees this in a problem: 'Rearrange d = rt to solve for t. Divide both sides by r: t = d/r. Now if you kno...' — what concept should they identify?
quizzes.explanation: This example illustrates Range: The set of all possible output values (y-values) the function can produce.

quizzes.q: A student sees this in a problem: 'The formula for the area of a triangle is A = ½bh. Solve for h. Multiply both si...' — what concept should they identify?
quizzes.explanation: This example illustrates formula: The formula is just geometry in algebraic form! Completing the square

quizzes.q: A student sees this in a problem: 'Rearrange F = (9/5)C + 32 to solve for C (Celsius-Fahrenheit conversion). Subtra...' — what concept should they identify?
quizzes.explanation: This example illustrates Range: The set of all possible output values (y-values) the function can produce.

quizzes.q: What is 25% of 200?
Solve: 3x + 5 = 20
quizzes.explanation: 3x + 5 = 20 → 3x = 15 → x = 5. Subtract 5 from both sides, then divide by 3.

quizzes.q: What is the square root of 144?
quizzes.explanation: √144 = 12 because 12 × 12 = 144. Note that (−12)² = 144 too, but the square root symbol denotes the principal (positive) root.

quizzes.q: A car travels 120 km in 2 hours. What is its speed?
quizzes.explanation: Speed = distance / time = 120 km / 2 hours = 60 km/h. This is the basic speed-distance-time relationship.

quizzes.q: Which is a prime number?
quizzes.explanation: 23 is prime because its only factors are 1 and 23. In contrast: 15 = 3×5, 21 = 3×7, 25 = 5×5.

quizzes.q: What is 2³?
quizzes.explanation: 2³ = 2 × 2 × 2 = 8. The exponent indicates repeated multiplication, not repeated addition.

quizzes.q: Factor: x² − 9
quizzes.explanation: x² − 9 is a difference of squares: a² − b² = (a−b)(a+b). Here a = x and b = 3, so x² − 9 = (x−3)(x+3).

quizzes.q: Solve: x² − 5x + 6 = 0
What is the slope-intercept form of a line?
quizzes.explanation: The slope-intercept form is y = mx + b, where m is the slope and b is the y-intercept. It's the most common way to write a linear equation.

quizzes.q: Simplify: (x³)(x⁴)
quizzes.explanation: When multiplying powers with the same base, add the exponents: x³ · x⁴ = x^(3+4) = x⁷. Don't multiply the exponents.

quizzes.q: What is the quadratic formula?
quizzes.explanation: The quadratic formula is x = (−b ± √(b²−4ac)) / 2a, derived from completing the square on ax² + bx + c = 0.

quizzes.q: Solve the inequality: 2x − 3 > 7
quizzes.explanation: Adding 3 to both sides: 2x > 10. Dividing by 2: x > 5.

quizzes.q: If 5x + 2y = 16 and x + 2y = 8, what is the value of x?
quizzes.explanation: The correct answer is "x = 2."

quizzes.q: Factor completely: x² + 7x + 12
quizzes.explanation: The correct answer is "(x + 3)(x + 4)."

quizzes.q: A shopkeeper buys an item for ₹400 and sells it for ₹500. What is the profit percentage?
quizzes.explanation: The correct answer is "25%."

quizzes.q: What are the roots of x² + 4x + 4 = 0?
quizzes.explanation: This factors as (x+2)² = 0, giving a repeated root x = −2.

quizzes.q: Simplify: (2x²y)³
quizzes.explanation: Apply the exponent to each factor: 2³ × (x²)³ × y³ = 8x⁶y³.

quizzes.q: The sum of two numbers is 30 and their difference is 10. What are the numbers?
quizzes.explanation: The correct answer is "20 and 10."

quizzes.q: 'is the branch of mathematics that uses letters (variables) to represent unknown ...' — What term is being described?
quizzes.explanation: 'Algebra' is defined as: is the branch of mathematics that uses letters (variables) to represent unknown values and describes relationships between quantities using equations and expressions.

quizzes.q: 'is an equation where the highest power of the variable is 1. Its graph is always...' — What term is being described?
quizzes.explanation: 'A linear equation' is defined as: is an equation where the highest power of the variable is 1. Its graph is always a straight line. The general form is ax + b = c, where a, b, and c are constants and a is not equal to 0.

quizzes.q: 'is an expression made up of terms that are constants or products of constants an...' — What term is being described?
quizzes.explanation: 'A polynomial' is defined as: is an expression made up of terms that are constants or products of constants and variables raised to whole-number exponents. Examples: 3x^2 + 2x - 1, 5y^3 - y, and 7 are all polynomials.

quizzes.q: 'is the reverse of expanding — it means writing a polynomial as a product of simp...' — What term is being described?
quizzes.explanation: 'Factoring' is defined as: is the reverse of expanding — it means writing a polynomial as a product of simpler expressions. It is the most important skill for solving quadratic equations and simplifying rational expressions on the CET.

quizzes.q: 'has the standard form ax^2 + bx + c = 0, where a is not equal to 0. The word 'qu...' — What term is being described?
quizzes.explanation: 'A quadratic equation' is defined as: has the standard form ax^2 + bx + c = 0, where a is not equal to 0. The word 'quadratic' comes from 'quad' (square) because the highest power is 2. Its graph is a parabola — a U-shaped curve.

quizzes.q: 'are a shorthand for repeated multiplication. In a^n, 'a' is the base and 'n' is ...' — What term is being described?
quizzes.explanation: 'Exponents' is defined as: are a shorthand for repeated multiplication. In a^n, 'a' is the base and 'n' is the exponent, meaning a multiplied by itself n times. Mastering exponent rules is essential because they appear in every CET math section.

quizzes.q: 'compares two expressions using <, >, <=, or >= instead of an equals sign. Solvin...' — What term is being described?
quizzes.explanation: 'An inequality' is defined as: compares two expressions using <, >, <=, or >= instead of an equals sign. Solving inequalities is similar to solving equations, but with one critical rule: when you multiply or divide both sides by a negative number, you must flip the inequality sign.

quizzes.q: 'is a set of two or more equations with the same variables. The solution is the p...' — What term is being described?
quizzes.explanation: 'A system of equations' is defined as: is a set of two or more equations with the same variables. The solution is the point (or points) where all equations are satisfied simultaneously. On the CET, systems typically involve 2 equations and 2 unknowns.

quizzes.q: Which term matches this description: 'has the standard form ax^2 + bx + c = 0, where a is not equal to 0. The word 'qu...'?
quizzes.explanation: 'A quadratic equation' is defined as: has the standard form ax^2 + bx + c = 0, where a is not equal to 0. The word 'quadratic' comes from 'quad' (square) because the highest power is 2. Its graph is a parabola — a U-shaped curve.

quizzes.q: Which term matches this description: 'are a shorthand for repeated multiplication. In a^n, 'a' is the base and 'n' is ...'?
quizzes.explanation: 'Exponents' is defined as: are a shorthand for repeated multiplication. In a^n, 'a' is the base and 'n' is the exponent, meaning a multiplied by itself n times. Mastering exponent rules is essential because they appear in every CET math section.

quizzes.q: Which term matches this description: 'compares two expressions using <, >, <=, or >= instead of an equals sign. Solvin...'?
quizzes.explanation: 'An inequality' is defined as: compares two expressions using <, >, <=, or >= instead of an equals sign. Solving inequalities is similar to solving equations, but with one critical rule: when you multiply or divide both sides by a negative number, you must flip the inequality sign.

quizzes.q: Which term matches this description: 'is a set of two or more equations with the same variables. The solution is the p...'?
quizzes.explanation: 'A system of equations' is defined as: is a set of two or more equations with the same variables. The solution is the point (or points) where all equations are satisfied simultaneously. On the CET, systems typically involve 2 equations and 2 unknowns.

quizzes.q: Which concept is best illustrated by the following: 'is the branch of mathematics that uses letters (variables) t...'?
quizzes.explanation: This describes Algebra: is the branch of mathematics that uses letters (variables) to represent unknown values and describes relationships between quantities using equations and expressions.

quizzes.q: Which concept is best illustrated by the following: 'is an equation where the highest power of the variable is 1....'?
quizzes.explanation: This describes A linear equation: is an equation where the highest power of the variable is 1. Its graph is always a straight line. The general form is ax + b = c, where a, b, and c are constants and a is not equal to 0.

quizzes.q: Which concept is best illustrated by the following: 'is an expression made up of terms that are constants or prod...'?
quizzes.explanation: This describes A polynomial: is an expression made up of terms that are constants or products of constants and variables raised to whole-number exponents. Examples: 3x^2 + 2x - 1, 5y^3 - y, and 7 are all polynomials.

quizzes.q: Which concept is best illustrated by the following: 'is the reverse of expanding — it means writing a polynomial ...'?
quizzes.explanation: This describes Factoring: is the reverse of expanding — it means writing a polynomial as a product of simpler expressions. It is the most important skill for solving quadratic equations and simplifying rational expressions on the CET.

quizzes.q: Which concept is best illustrated by the following: 'has the standard form ax^2 + bx + c = 0, where a is not equa...'?
quizzes.explanation: This describes A quadratic equation: has the standard form ax^2 + bx + c = 0, where a is not equal to 0. The word 'quadratic' comes from 'quad' (square) because the highest power is 2. Its graph is a parabola — a U-shaped curve.

quizzes.q: Which concept is best illustrated by the following: 'are a shorthand for repeated multiplication. In a^n, 'a' is ...'?
quizzes.explanation: This describes Exponents: are a shorthand for repeated multiplication. In a^n, 'a' is the base and 'n' is the exponent, meaning a multiplied by itself n times. Mastering exponent rules is essential because they appear in every CET math section.

quizzes.q: Which concept is best illustrated by the following: 'compares two expressions using <, >, <=, or >= instead of an...'?
quizzes.explanation: This describes An inequality: compares two expressions using <, >, <=, or >= instead of an equals sign. Solving inequalities is similar to solving equations, but with one critical rule: when you multiply or divide both sides by a negative number, you must flip the inequality sign.

quizzes.q: Which concept is best illustrated by the following: 'is a set of two or more equations with the same variables. T...'?
quizzes.explanation: This describes A system of equations: is a set of two or more equations with the same variables. The solution is the point (or points) where all equations are satisfied simultaneously. On the CET, systems typically involve 2 equations and 2 unknowns.

quizzes.q: A student sees this in a problem: 'Example: Expand (2x - 3)^2. This means (2x - 3)(2x - 3). FOIL: 4x^2 - 6x - 6x + ...' — what concept should they identify?
quizzes.explanation: This example illustrates Mean (Average): The sum of all values divided by the number of values. Formula: x̄ = Σx / n. The most common measure of center, but sensitive to outliers.

quizzes.q: A student sees this in a problem: 'Example 2: Rationalize 1/(sqrt(3) - 1). Multiply by conjugate (sqrt(3) + 1)/(sqr...' — what concept should they identify?
quizzes.explanation: This example illustrates Rational function: f(x) = P(x)/Q(x) where P and Q are polynomials. Domain excludes values where Q(x) = 0.

quizzes.q: What is the range of f(x) = x²?
quizzes.explanation: f(x) = x² is always ≥ 0 since squaring any real number gives a non-negative result. So the range is y ≥ 0.

quizzes.q: A function passes the vertical line test. What does this mean?
quizzes.explanation: The vertical line test: if any vertical line crosses the graph more than once, it's not a function. This ensures each x-value maps to exactly one y-value.

quizzes.q: What is the domain of f(x) = 1/x?
quizzes.explanation: For f(x) = 1/x, division by zero is undefined, so x = 0 is excluded from the domain. All other real numbers are valid inputs.

quizzes.q: If f(x) = 2x + 1, what is f(3)?
quizzes.explanation: f(3) = 2(3) + 1 = 6 + 1 = 7. Substitute x = 3 into the function and simplify.

quizzes.q: If f(x) = x² − 1, what is f(−2)?
quizzes.explanation: f(−2) = (−2)² − 1 = 4 − 1 = 3. Be careful: (−2)² = 4, not −4. The square of a negative number is positive.

quizzes.q: What is the domain of f(x) = √x?
quizzes.explanation: The square root function requires non-negative input: x ≥ 0. Taking the square root of a negative number is not defined in the reals.

quizzes.q: The range of f(x) = x² is:
quizzes.explanation: Since x² ≥ 0 for all real x, f(x) = x² can never be negative. The minimum is 0 (at x = 0), so the range is y ≥ 0.

quizzes.q: f(x) = 2x + 3 is a:
quizzes.explanation: f(x) = 2x + 3 is linear because the highest power of x is 1. Its graph is a straight line with slope 2 and y-intercept 3.

quizzes.q: The vertex of f(x) = x² - 4x + 3 is:
quizzes.explanation: Complete the square or use x = −b/(2a) = 4/2 = 2. Then f(2) = 4 − 8 + 3 = −1. Vertex is (2, −1).

quizzes.q: An even function satisfies:
quizzes.explanation: An even function is symmetric about the y-axis: f(−x) = f(x). Examples include x² and cos(x).

quizzes.q: An odd function satisfies:
quizzes.explanation: An odd function has rotational symmetry about the origin: f(−x) = −f(x). Examples include x³ and sin(x).

quizzes.q: The composition f(g(x)) means:
quizzes.explanation: f(g(x)) means first apply g to x, then apply f to the result. The inner function is evaluated first.

quizzes.q: The inverse of f(x) = x³ is:
quizzes.explanation: To find the inverse of f(x) = x³, solve y = x³ for x: x = y^(1/3). So f⁻¹(x) = x^(1/3) (cube root).

quizzes.q: 'A function is a rule that assigns exactly ONE output to each input. Think of it ...' — What term is being described?
quizzes.explanation: 'Function' is defined as: A function is a rule that assigns exactly ONE output to each input. Think of it as a machine: you feed in a number (input), the machine applies a rule, and out comes exactly one result (output).

quizzes.q: 'A graph represents a function if and only if every vertical line intersects the ...' — What term is being described?
quizzes.explanation: 'Vertical Line Test' is defined as: A graph represents a function if and only if every vertical line intersects the graph at most once. If any vertical line hits the graph twice, it is NOT a function.

quizzes.q: 'The set of all valid input values (x-values) for which the function is defined.' — What term is being described?
quizzes.explanation: 'Domain' is defined as: The set of all valid input values (x-values) for which the function is defined.

quizzes.q: 'The set of all possible output values (y-values) the function can produce.' — What term is being described?
quizzes.explanation: 'Range' is defined as: The set of all possible output values (y-values) the function can produce.

quizzes.q: 'f(x) = mx + b, where m = slope (steepness) and b = y-intercept (where it crosses...' — What term is being described?
quizzes.explanation: 'Linear function' is defined as: f(x) = mx + b, where m = slope (steepness) and b = y-intercept (where it crosses the y-axis). Graph is a straight line.

quizzes.q: 'f(x) = ax² + bx + c (a ≠ 0). Graph is a parabola. If a > 0, opens upward (∪ shap...' — What term is being described?
quizzes.explanation: 'Quadratic function' is defined as: f(x) = ax² + bx + c (a ≠ 0). Graph is a parabola. If a > 0, opens upward (∪ shape). If a < 0, opens downward (∩ shape).

quizzes.q: 'f(x) = aₙxⁿ + aₙ₋₁xⁿ⁻¹ + ... + a₁x + a₀. The highest power n is the degree. Degr...' — What term is being described?
quizzes.explanation: 'Polynomial function' is defined as: f(x) = aₙxⁿ + aₙ₋₁xⁿ⁻¹ + ... + a₁x + a₀. The highest power n is the degree. Degree determines shape: degree 1 = line, degree 2 = parabola, degree 3 = cubic (S-curve).

quizzes.q: 'f(x) = P(x)/Q(x) where P and Q are polynomials. Domain excludes values where Q(x...' — What term is being described?
quizzes.explanation: 'Rational function' is defined as: f(x) = P(x)/Q(x) where P and Q are polynomials. Domain excludes values where Q(x) = 0.

quizzes.q: 'f(x) = a · bˣ where b > 0 and b ≠ 1. If b > 1, it's growth (rapidly increasing)....' — What term is being described?
quizzes.explanation: 'Exponential function' is defined as: f(x) = a · bˣ where b > 0 and b ≠ 1. If b > 1, it's growth (rapidly increasing). If 0 < b < 1, it's decay (rapidly decreasing).

quizzes.q: 'log_b(x) = y means bʸ = x. It is the inverse of exponential. 'log base b of x eq...' — What term is being described?
quizzes.explanation: 'Logarithm' is defined as: log_b(x) = y means bʸ = x. It is the inverse of exponential. 'log base b of x equals the power you raise b to get x.'

quizzes.q: '(f ∘ g)(x) = f(g(x)). Read as 'f of g of x.' First apply g to x, then apply f to...' — What term is being described?
quizzes.explanation: 'Composition' is defined as: (f ∘ g)(x) = f(g(x)). Read as 'f of g of x.' First apply g to x, then apply f to the result. Order matters! f(g(x)) ≠ g(f(x)) in general.

quizzes.q: 'f⁻¹(x) 'undoes' what f does. If f(a) = b, then f⁻¹(b) = a. The inverse reverses ...' — What term is being described?
quizzes.explanation: 'Inverse function' is defined as: f⁻¹(x) 'undoes' what f does. If f(a) = b, then f⁻¹(b) = a. The inverse reverses the input-output relationship. f(f⁻¹(x)) = x and f⁻¹(f(x)) = x.

quizzes.q: 'A function defined by different rules for different parts of its domain. Like a ...' — What term is being described?
quizzes.explanation: 'Piecewise function' is defined as: A function defined by different rules for different parts of its domain. Like a choose-your-own-adventure: if x < 0, use rule A; if x ≥ 0, use rule B.

quizzes.explanation: This describes Function: A function is a rule that assigns exactly ONE output to each input. Think of it as a machine: you feed in a number (input), the machine applies a rule, and out comes exactly one result (output).

quizzes.q: A student encounters 'A graph represents a function if and only if every...' — which concept does this illustrate?
quizzes.explanation: This describes Vertical Line Test: A graph represents a function if and only if every vertical line intersects the graph at most once. If any vertical line hits the graph twice, it is NOT a function.

quizzes.q: A student encounters 'The set of all valid input values (x-values) for w...' — which concept does this illustrate?
quizzes.explanation: This describes Domain: The set of all valid input values (x-values) for which the function is defined.

quizzes.q: A student encounters 'The set of all possible output values (y-values) t...' — which concept does this illustrate?
